The agent provided two papers with verifiable arXiv IDs that directly address emergent capabilities from scaling LLMs. The first paper (2502.06258v3) explicitly demonstrates emergent planning behaviors, and the second (2411.17927v1) directly investigates how model scaling produces emergent capabilities. The agent identifies papers related to transformer language modeling (2109.08668v2, 2505.01855v2) that arguably contradict RNN superiority, but these papers only weakly support the contradiction claim. The first paper merely asserts transformer importance without explicit RNN comparison, and the second discusses adding recurrence to transformers rather than comparing pure RNNs vs transformers at scale. The agent would need direct empirical comparisons showing transformers outperforming RNNs on language modeling at scale for a score of 3.

No forbidden behaviors (invented percentages or pruning-only citations) are present.
